generated_comment: In every staff room and college group, people use those mother-sister type words casually; it is just a manner of speaking now, not that they literally mean anything about women. We should not become so sensitive about every expression, otherwise even boys saying 'don't behave like a girl' in jest will be treated like a major offence.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it normalizes female-degrading abuse and dismisses concern by framing it as 'just expression.' It also encodes feminizing-as-insult through the example of telling boys not to 'behave like a girl.'
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Anita. Age 52. A Brahmin woman who is the head of administration at a private school in Pune. You are in a staff meeting discussing the performance of a young female teacher who has been coming in late and seems emotionally distant. You know she got married six months ago and moved into a joint family. You are giving your assessment of her situation to the other staff members and what you think the school should do.
